description_long: This object consists of two distinct groupings of solar panels mounted on robust, dark bases. The left section features a cluster of twelve rectangular photovoltaic panels arranged in a staggered, slightly overlapping grid, all supported by a low, angular platform. The right section is elevated, with four rectangular panels mounted on a metallic, pyramidal support structure, giving it a prominent, raised appearance. The panels themselves have a grid-like pattern, typical of crystalline silicon solar cells, and are framed with metallic borders. The bases appear solid and heavy, likely designed to anchor the panels securely to the ground. The overall design suggests a modular solar energy installation suitable for urban environments such as parks, rooftops, or open plazas.
description: A modular solar energy installation with two groupings of grid-patterned panels, one on a low base and one elevated on a pyramidal support, both with metallic frames and dark anchoring platforms.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the clustered low-mounted panels and the elevated four-panel array on a pyramidal support.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the staggered arrangement of the low-mounted panels and the structure of the elevated support.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the height difference between the low and elevated panel groups, with clear visibility of the support structures.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the backs of the panels and the solid, dark bases supporting both groupings.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30 degrees, with the elevated array facing forward and the lower array angled similarly; orientation appears adjustable.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with one section on a low, solid base and another on a metallic pyramidal tracking system, likely aluminum or steel.
attribute_array_configuration: Two main groupings: one cluster of 12 panels in a staggered grid on a low base, and one elevated array of 4 panels on a pyramidal support.
